person.name: Christian Latimer
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Francis Blomefield, the historian of Norfolk, is the source of many other accounts and his chapter concerning Fersfield he explained as follows:

"Sir Robert de Bosco, who succeeded in 1298, being then 30 years old, at which time he had two fees, which formerly were the Bygods, one of which was in Smalburgh, and the other here and another in Fersfield, held of the Abbot of St. Edmund. He married Christian Le Latimer, daughter of Sir William Latimer, and widow of Sir John Carbonel of Waldingfield, in Suffolk, who was her first husband, by whom she had William Carbonel, her son and heir to his father. Upon Sir Robert's marriage, he settled this manor upon her for life, which she held some time after his death, for it appears by the Institution Books, that she presented in 1316; and the year after Sir Robert's death, which was in 1311, she married a third time, to Sir Thomas Mose, Knt. by whom she had a daughter, Mariot, married to Sir William Botevile, or Bovile, Knt. Some pedigrees that I have seen make this Thomas (or Robert Moose, Knt. as he is called in some deeds) her first husband, but I am certain he was her last, for in 1312, the very year that she married Sir Thomas, she presented here by the name of Christian de Mose, late wife of Sir Robert de Bosco, Knt. He, in 1308, held the third part of the manor of Carbonels, in Waldingfield-Magna, in Suffolk, with the advowsons of that church, and Chiston, in dower, in right of the said Christian, of the inheritance of William Carbonel, on which William the manor of Fersfield was settled in tail, if they had no issue, as was the manor of Walton, and the advowsons. In 1308, they conveyed the manor of Burston to this William and his heirs. Sir Robert, at his death in 1311, was seized of Fersfield, Denton, Garboldisham, &c. leaving Robert du Boys, Knt. his only son and heir, then very young, and one daughter, Alice. "

It can also be noted that Blomefield equated William de Botevile or Bovile with William de Buttevilleyn (whose name was spelled many ways). This he explains in his chapter concerning the manor of Flroden, saying that in 1292, "Sir William Butteveleyn, Knt. had the manor of Florden, and:

"his wife Margaret was daughter and only heir, of Sir Thomas Mose, first husband of Christian, daughter of Sir William Latimer, Knt.; he released to Sir Robert son of John de Thorp, divers of his villeins, which lived in Thorp, but belonged to this manor, and consented that Sir Peter Rosceline, Knt. should convey to the said Sir John Thorp, Knt. a meadow held of him in Flordon: in 1305, he held this and Flixton at two fees."

Christian Latimer (about 1280 Corby, Northamptonshire- 1317)

«b»Parents«/b»
Father: Sir William Latimer (Latymer), 1st Baron Latimer of Corby (d.1305)
Mother: Amicia (Alicia) Ledet

«b»Marriage and Issue«/b»
m.1 Sir John Carbonel of Wadingfield, co. Suffolk

m.2 Robert de Bosco (d.1311) (Father: Robert du Bois)

m.3 Sir Thomas Mose. 

«b»Issue:«/b»
1.) Mariotta Mose (b. aft. 1312) m. Sir William Bovill

«b»Property«/b»
Fersfield

«b»Timeline«/b»
1268, Robert born in England, s/o Robert du Bois.

1298, Robert, age 30, heir to his father with fees in Smalburgh and Fersfield.

1294, Christian born in England, d/o Sir William le Latimer & Lucy de Thweng.

Christian married 1st to Sir John Carbonel of Wadingfield, co. Suffolk.

25 December 1305, "Debtor: Robert de Boys Bosco, knight, of Norfolk [held Little Ellingham, Wayland Hundred, Norfolk." (S) UKNA.

1307, Lands called Sanford and other lands in Stonfold, held by Lord Robert de Boys.

25 February 1308, Edward II crowned

1308, Robert married Christian and settled Fersfield on her for life. In dower of Christian he held a third part of the manor of Carbonels in Waldingfield-Magna, co. Suffolk.

1308, Robert and Christiana conveyed the manor of Burston on her son William Carbonels.

1310, "Debtor: Robert de Boys de Bosco knight, of Norfolk and of Suffolk [held manor of Fersfield in Diss Hundred]."

1310, Deed of attornment by Robert de Bosco and Christian his wife [who was the wife of Sir John Carbonel, kt.] to Thos. Carbonel and Elizabeth his wife.

1311, Robert "du Bosco" died.

1312, Christian married 3rd Sir Thomas Mose.

1312, Christian de Mose, late the wife of Sir Robert de Bosco, Knt., presented at Fersfield.

1316, Christian presented at Fersfield.

i. Robert de Bois, born 1309 in England. 1333, Robert Robert, knt. of Fersfield and Garboldisham, Norfolk, and Brokes, Suffolk, died; his sister Alice his heir.

ii. Alice de Boys, born by 1311 in England.

Child of Thomas and Christian:

i. Mariotta Mose, born aft. 1312 in England. Mariotta married Sir William Bovill. Immediate Family: Daughter of William le Latimer, 1st Baron Latimer of Corby and Alicia Ledet, Heiress of Corby

Wife of Robert De Boys Mother of Sir Robert de Boys and Alice de Babington. Sister of Joan le Latimer; John De Latimer, Lord and William le Latimer IV, 2nd Baron of Corby.

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: Christian
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

variants.name: Christianne
meanings: From Latin Christianus ‘follower of Christ’, in use as a given name from the Middle Ages onwards, especially as a girl's name. The name of Christ (Greek Khristos) is a translation of the Hebrew term Messiah ‘anointed’.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

en.lifeSummary: When Christian Latimer was born in 1269, in Corby, Northamptonshire, England, her father, William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby, was 28 and her mother, Alice Ledet, was 21. She married Thomas Mose after 1312. She died about 1320, in Suffolk, England, at the age of 52.

Elle a épousé Thomas Mose après 1312. Elle est décédée environ 1320, à Suffolk, Angleterre, à l’âge de 52 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Christian Latimer(이)가 1269년 영국 노르섬프토실르 코르비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William le Latimer 1st Baron Latimer of Corby(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Ledet(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1312년 이후 Thomas Mose(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 대략 1320년 영국 수폴크에서 52세의 나이로 사망했다.
